properties.name: Epoch 2 · Military radar path
properties.desc: Primary (military) radar track: the turnback at IGARI, recrossing the Malay peninsula near Kota Bharu, passing south of Penang, then northwest up the Strait of Malacca to the last fix 10 NM past waypoint MEKAR at 02:22:12 UTC+8. Recorded but lower precision; no transponder was operating.

citation.label: Malaysian SIR (2018); ATSB Definition of Underwater Search Areas

points.label: Start of primary-radar-only epoch (same point as last secondary return)
Military-derived track resumes just past IGARI; transponder off, turn beginning
points.source: Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP numeric track (times are indicative labels; position published)

points.label: Easternmost point of the turnback - the aircraft continued ~15 NM toward BITOD before reversing
Northernmost point of the turnback hook, turning left through north onto a southwesterly track
Established southwest-bound back toward the Malay peninsula
Kota Bharu civil primary radar acquires the target. FI: 01:30:35 (UTC+8)-01:35 (UTC+8) heading 231M, ground speed 496 kt, 'registered height of 35,700 ft' (military; altitude disclaimed by SIR)
points.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_IN (recorded, ~4 s scans, decimated); FI p3 verbatim altitude quote

points.label: FI: 01:36 (UTC+8)-01:36:40 (UTC+8) 'height fluctuation between 31,100 and 33,000 ft' (military; disclaimed)
points.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_IN (recorded); FI p3 altitude quote

points.label: Crossing the coast just north of Kota Bharu; last inbound PSR return before the radar's overhead cone (FI coast-crossing time 01:37:22 (UTC+8))
points.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_IN final return (recorded); FI p7 via Gilbert PDF

points.label: Kota Bharu PSR reacquires outbound (2-minute overhead gap)
FI: 01:39:59 (UTC+8) heading 244M, ground speed 529 kt, 'height at 32,800 ft' (military; disclaimed)
points.source: ATC-Caption-2025 KB_PSR_OUT (recorded); FI p3 altitude quote

points.label: Last Kota Bharu PSR return, crossing the peninsula toward Penang
Butterworth civil primary radar acquires the target
Butterworth track (data gap ~01:48 (UTC+8)-01:51 (UTC+8) follows)
Approaching Penang Island from the northeast
South of Penang Island (FI: Kota Bharu-captured target P3426 dropped 01:52:35 (UTC+8) ~6 NM south of Penang; SIR quotes a disclaimed 44,700 ft here)
points.source: ATC-Caption-2025 BW_PSR corrected (recorded); FI p7/p23, SIR p50

points.label: Right turn onto ~290° up the Strait of Malacca
Last civil primary radar return (Butterworth). SIR: military blip near Pulau Perak at 1801:59 at a disclaimed 4,800 ft
points.source: ATC-Caption-2025 BW_PSR corrected final return (recorded); SIR p50

points.label: Last point of the 10-second military-derived track. SIR: the blip disappeared at 1803:09
points.source: Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP numeric track final 10-s point; SIR p50

points.label: RADAR GAP (SIR: no returns 02:03:09 (UTC+8)-02:15:25 (UTC+8)) - straight-line interpolation toward airway N571
Passing waypoint VAMPI on N571 (FI-timed anchor inside the radar gap)
points.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position: OpenNav VAMPI (6°10'56"N 97°35'08"E)

points.label: Along N571 VAMPI-MEKAR (military radar reacquired 02:15:25 (UTC+8) per SIR; no numeric positions published for this segment)
Passing north of waypoint MEKAR (MEKAR at 6.5039N 96.4911E)
points.source: time: FI p7 via Gilbert PDF; position interpolated ~7 NM past MEKAR along N571 toward NILAM (MEKAR/NILAM from OpenNav)

points.label: Last primary radar fix, 10 NM past MEKAR on N571 toward NILAM. SIR: '195 nm from Butterworth, heading 285°, 516 kt, 29,500 ft' (altitude disclaimed). Corroborated by the KL-LSTRP numeric track's final row 6.5999N 96.3111E (~2.8 km away); Lido-slide alternative 6.871123N 97.354047E not used
points.source: time: FI p7 / SIR p50; position: SIR 10-NM-past-MEKAR interpretation as computed by mh370search (Iannello); cross-check: Geoscience/ATSB KL-LSTRP xlsx final row
